{{small|Leigh Leopards, 6 March}}{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Hull FC earn rare Super League draw with Leigh Leopards after spirited fightback |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match-reports/hull-fc-earn-rare-super-10001166 |access-date=6 March 2025 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=6 March 2025}} | top_tries = [[Lewis Martin (rugby league)|Lewis Martin]] (25) | top_goals = [[Aiden Sezer]] (32) | top_points = [[Lewis Martin (rugby league)|Lewis Martin]] (100) | prev_season = [[2024 Hull FC season|2024]] | next_season = [[2026 Hull FC season|2026]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Hull F.C. seasons | pattern_la1 = | pattern_b1 =_blackhoops | pattern_ra1 = | pattern_sh1 = | pattern_so1 =_blacktop | leftarm1 = ffffff | body1 = ffffff | rightarm1 = ffffff | shorts1 = ffffff | socks1 = ffffff | pattern_la2 = _navyborder | pattern_b2 = | pattern_ra2 = _navyborder | pattern_sh2 = _bluesides | pattern_so2 = | leftarm2 = 00ffff | body2 = 00ffff | rightarm2 = 00ffff | shorts2 = 00ffff | socks2 = 00ffff }} The 2025 season was '''[[Hull F.C.]]''''s 28th consecutive season playing in England's top division of [[rugby league]]. They competed in the [[2025 Super League season]] and the [[2025 Challenge Cup]]. The 2025 season saw major changes in the club's management. Following the sacking of head coach [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] and assistant coach [[Stanley Gene]] during the [[2024 Hull FC season|2024 season]], Hull FC announced that former [[Brisbane Broncos]] assistant coach [[John Cartwright (rugby league)|John Cartwright]] would be the club's head coach from the 2025 season.{{cite news |title=Hull FC announce the appointment of John Cartwright as head coach from next season |url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12210/13140912/hull-fc-announce-the-appointment-of-john-cartwright-as-head-coach-from-next-season |access-date=19 October 2024 |work=Sky Sports |date=21 May 2024}} [[Andy Last]] was also announced to be returning from the [[England national rugby league team]] to be Hull FC's assistant coach, having previously left the club in November 2020.{{cite news |last1=Jackson |first1=Will |title=Hull FC make key addition as John Cartwright's staff comes together |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/hull-fc-make-key-addition-as-john-cartwrights-staff-comes-together-96618/ |work=Serious About Rugby League |access-date=19 October 2024 |date=5 September 2024}} On 13 November 2024, chairman [[Adam Pearson (sports executive)|Adam Pearson]] announced that he had sold Hull F.C. to businessmen Andrew Thirkill and David Hood and was stepping down as chairman after 13 years in charge of the club.{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=Hull FC announce change in ownership as successful Yorkshire businessman takes over Super League club |url=https://www.totalrl.com/hull-fc-announce-change-in-ownership-as-successful-yorkshire-businessman-takes-over-super-league-club/ |access-date=21 November 2024 |work=Total Rugby League |date=13 November 2024}} ==Preseason friendlies== {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col3center col6center col9right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!Report |- style= | 1 February; ===Table=== {{2025 Super League regular season table|showteam=HUL}} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2025 Challenge Cup}} On 25 June 2024, the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] announced a change to the Challenge Cup format, totalling 7 rounds compared to the previous 9, with Super League teams entering to play away from home at round 3.{{Cite web |date=25 June 2024 |title=New format for Betfred Challenge Cup in 2025 |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62890/new-format-for-betfred-challenge-cup-in-2025 |access-date=19 October 2024 |website=[[Rugby Football League]]}} Hull F.C. were drawn on 14 January to play [[York Acorn A.R.L.F.C.]], who play in the [[National Conference League Premier Division]], following fixtures in Rounds 1 and 2 that eliminated [[Great Britain Police Rugby League|GB Police]], the [[London Chargers]] and the [[Oulton Raiders]] from contention.{{cite news |title=Holders Wigan face possible Sheffield tie in cup |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cj48vxx28rlo |access-date=14 January 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=14 January 2025}}{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=BBC make Challenge Cup Third Round broadcast pick as full schedule locked in |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-third-round-schedule-2025 |access-date=28 January 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=28 January 2025}} The fixture was originally set to be played away at the Acorn Sport and Social Club on Thanet Road, [[York]], however the venue was changed to [[Featherstone Rovers]]' [[Post Office Road]] ground due to the expense involved in York Acorn preparing their ground for Hull F.C.{{cite news |last1=Kennedy |first1=Emma |title=York Acorn boss on moving Hull FC Challenge Cup tie to Featherstone |url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/24911879.york-acorn-boss-moving-hull-fc-challenge-cup-tie-featherstone/ |access-date=8 February 2025 |work=[[The Press (York)|The Press]] |date=5 February 2025 |location=York}} Following their victory against York, Hull F.C. were drawn on 15 February to play [[Wigan Warriors]] away at the [[Brick Community Stadium]] on 15 March.{{cite news |last1=Olawumi |first1=Ben |title=Challenge Cup Fourth Round draw as 11 Super League sides discover opponents |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-fourth-round-draw-as-11-super-league-sides-discover-opponents |access-date=15 February 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=15 February 2025}}{{cite web |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round Four – broadcast coverage confirmed |url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/63473/betfred-challenge-cup-round-four-%E2%80%93-broadcast-coverage-confirmed |website=rugby-league.com |access-date=24 February 2025 |date=19 February 2025}} Following their 26–22 comeback victory against Wigan, Hull F.C. entered into the quarter-finals and, for the first time since they were knocked out in first round of the [[1985–86 Challenge Cup]], were drawn in a [[Hull Derby]] to play [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at home on 5 April.{{cite news |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron |title=Challenge Cup quarter-final draw: Hull FC to host Hull KR in mouthwatering last eight |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-quarter-final-draw-hull-fc-to-host-hull-kr-in-mouthwatering-last-eight |access-date=17 March 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=17 March 2025}}{{cite news |title=BBC to show Hull derby and Wire v Saints cup ties |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cx2r1gpyl43o |access-date=18 March 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=18 March 2025}} Hull F.C. were ultimately knocked out of the 2025 Challenge Cup during this fixture.{{cite news |last1=Tomlinson |first1=Daniel |title=Hull KR weather Hull FC storm to record derby victory and seal semi-final spot |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match-reports/hull-kr-weather-hull-fc-10083729 |access-date=5 April 2025 |work=Hull Daily
